FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Bank earnings set to boom on SpaceX fees and volatility, but Fed rate-hike odds rising on geopolitical tension could compress NIMs.

bullets.text: Q2 bank revenues poised to surge on SpaceX IPO advisory fees, geopolitical trading flows, and commercial lending pickup.
July Fed rate-hike odds climbing after Strait of Hormuz oil spike—direct hit to NIM compression and deposit costs for banks.
Trump's CFPB pick Brian Johnson gets 60-day review window post-confirmation to reshape staff-cut strategy, reducing near-term regulatory uncertainty.
Fed's Waller signals inflation broadening beyond energy/tariffs, keeping rate-hike door open despite recent disinflation trends.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Russell Investments goes non-profit under CalPERS in a landmark $580B pension acquisition, while RIA consolidation accelerates with leadership transitions and channel restructuring across the sector.

bullets.text: CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments in transformational deal; Russell sheds debt burden and adopts non-profit governance model similar to Vanguard's playbook for reputational lift.
RIA consolidation heats up: Hightower posts record organic growth, ex-Fido CTO raises $7.7M for standalone platform, Choreo closes fourth acquisition, and Wealthfront crosses $100B AUM.
Summitry ($4B RIA owned by Aspen PE) promotes President Katz and COO Wilkes to co-CEOs as founder Higgins transitions to executive chairman after 22 years.
Carson Group restructures go-to-market into separate W-2 and independent RIA channels to sharpen focus and drive organic growth amid consolidation wave.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Brazil's insurance market opens for business, Mile Auto consolidates MGAs, Tylenol litigation explodes, and Marsh/D&B advance distribution infrastructure.

bullets.text: Brazil's new Insurance Law takes effect with regulatory tailwinds—Uplift CEO signals reinsurer appetite on improved claims handling and contractual clarity.
Mile Auto acquires Insurance House (~$100M premium, 55k policyholders) effective July 2026, hitting 10 states and 1,600 distribution partners in AI-driven MGA consolidation play.
Appeals court revives 500+ Tylenol-autism lawsuits against Kenvue on July 13, overturning dismissal and creating major litigation tail for consumer health insurers.
Marsh launches MLOne, a US lead umbrella casualty facility to coordinate excess claims—Dun & Bradstreet simultaneously embeds business intelligence into Claude for real-time underwriting data.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Mastercard considers Vocalink exit; CMBS stress signals property distress; Nubank expands into Mexico; regulatory headwinds hit Klarna.

bullets.text: Mastercard exploring sale of Vocalink, the UK payments platform acquired in 2016—potential exit from UK banking infrastructure play as strategic review underway.
CMBS special servicing rates climbed in June 2026 driven by distress in retail, office, and lodging—widening credit deterioration signals workout pressure ahead for servicers.
Nubank cleared for full banking ops in Mexico after regulatory approval—LatAm fintech scaling digital banking footprint in key market.
Klarna hit with Dutch class action lawsuit over BNPL practices—regulatory/litigation pressure mounting for fintech unicorn in Europe.

PE & Deals.lede: Arctos deploys novel GP financing structure while Moelis and peers capitalize on booming LP-led secondaries; Korea's pension signals domestic PE appetite.

bullets.text: Arctos closed $6.2B GP financing using 'self-liquidating' Keystone structure—a debut in GP-financing market that could reshape how large funds access capital.
Moelis hired Evercore MD to build LP-led secondaries practice as major players (LGT, Dawson, Pomona) race to capture deals in expanding segment.
Korean pension KBIZ committing ~$230M to domestic blind-pool PE/VC funds in 2026, signaling institutional capital flow back to Korea-focused managers.

Markets & Macro.lede: Banking fees surging toward 4-year highs amid M&A revival, but margin debt climbing signals late-cycle risk as earnings season kicks off.

bullets.text: US investment banking fees tracking highest annual total since 2022, driven by M&A, IPO recovery, and AI-fueled debt markets—good news for fee pools.
Margin debt climbing as investors lever up to chase returns; Wall Street flagging it as a late-cycle greed signal that often precedes corrections.
Bank earnings kick off Tuesday with high expectations already priced in—watch for disappointment risk and what management guidance signals for FIG outlooks.
EU imported record Russian LNG in H1 2026 before scheduled ban, signaling energy-supply fragility with potential inflation implications for European financials.

Newsletters.lede: Big bank earnings kick off tomorrow with macro implications; meanwhile, ETF flows turn negative and China's AI push gains ground.

bullets.text: Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow—expect detailed reads on credit quality, deposits, and consumer health across lending and trading.
ETF flows went negative in a rare week; redemptions hit equity, bond, and alternative products—worth monitoring for broader sentiment shift.
Alibaba's AI model ranked first globally with cheaper alternatives; Chinese tech is eroding US dominance in artificial intelligence.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Five stories spanning AI competition, economic theory, and policy literacy—what determines winners in markets and policy preferences.

bullets.text: Open-source AI now matches frontier models at 1/10th the cost, forcing a shift from model capability to durable moats—relevant as enterprise AI investment decisions tighten.

Tyler Cowen's contrarian take: markets stay competitive only if P ≠ NP; if collusion becomes computationally detectable, cartels become sustainable—a theoretical frame worth tracking in antitrust debates.
Economic literacy, not self-interest, predicts policy support across the EU (free trade, immigration)—suggests educated coalitions, not just winners/losers, shape regulatory outcomes.

all.lede: Bank earnings season kicks off amid M&A momentum; Mastercard explores Vocalink exit while CalPERS snaps up Russell Investments.

bullets.text: Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow, offering a real-time read on credit quality, deposit health, and consumer spending across all major business lines.
US investment banking fees tracking for highest annual haul since 2022, driven by M&A revival, IPO recovery, and capital markets activity fueled by AI momentum.
CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments for governance restructure—the $580B pension system gains multi-asset platform while Russell sheds debt and adopts non-profit model.
Mastercard exploring sale of Vocalink, the UK payments platform acquired in 2016, potentially returning it to original UK bank owners.
